transcript.interviewer: What's a decision you keep coming back to?
transcript.interviewee: Whether to stay in the city or move back home. I've been going in circles on it for two years.
Everything I've built is here — my friends, the work I love, the person I've become. Leaving would mean walking away from all of it.
Part of me aches for it. My family's getting older, the pace is human, I could finally breathe. There's a whole life waiting there that I keep not living.
transcript.interviewer: Could you find a way to have both?
transcript.interviewee: That's the cruel part — I can't. Every month I stay is a month I'm not there. Choosing one doesn't pause the other, it kills it. I'm genuinely torn, and I hate that I haven't picked.
Honestly, neither. Both buttons look equally good and equally impossible. That's exactly why I'm stuck.
